title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: R package artfishr implements tools producing catch effort estimates sample-based survey data small-scales fisheries data following FAO ARTFISH extrapolation methodology. ensure consistency interoperability, input datasets must comply standardized formats validation rules. vignette describes: required data formats used artfishr, sample data format specifications provided package, generate empty templates input data, validate input data. Four core datasets supported: Active vessels: Number active vessels per stratum (vessels can potentially go fishing) Effort (two variants depending data collection method): fisher interview boat counting Active days: number days month fishing activity Landings: landings survey observation data

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: dataset type follows standardized structure defined JSON specification file. schemas describe column names, units, validation rules. example file can accessed directly via system.file(): Preview structure: specification file describes field required data structure.

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: (Text improve including effort type cases active_days need ) Effort data can collected two distinct monitoring approaches: Data obtained directly fishers questionnaires template effort.csv required (number days fished within period reference) case, active days survey necessary. Total number days month used default. Data collected via registers direct observation Template y (complete) required (number boats comparison total potentially active boat) number active days month must provided. can come active days survey estimation based fisheries knowledge. formats supported artfishr can validated using validation interface.

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: artfishr allows users programmatically generate empty templates dataset, based JSON specification. package provides generic method create_artfish_template() generate templates using dataset names listed . can also export template use argument save_as: use dedicated functions dataset :

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: artfishr includes validation utilities ensure datasets comply expected structure logical rules. global validator provided: function: - checks required fields dataset flags missing unexpected columns, - checks field values satisfy validation rules, - ensures consistency . Example: function returns structured report validation results messages. artfishr also includes specific validators allow running checks per dataset type.
code: validate_input_datasets(   active_vessels,   effort,   effort_source = c("fisher_interview", "boat_counting"),   active_days = NULL,   landings ) # Load sample datasets active_vessels <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/active_vessels.csv", package = "artfishr")) effort <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/effort.csv", package = "artfishr")) landings <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/landings.csv", package = "artfishr"))  # Validate all datasets validate_input_datasets(   active_vessels = active_vessels,   effort = effort,   effort_source = "fisher_interview",   landings = landings ) # Load sample datasets active_vessels <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/active_vessels.csv", package = "artfishr")) effort <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/effort.csv", package = "artfishr")) landings <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/landings.csv", package = "artfishr")) active_days <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata/samples/active_days.csv", package = "artfishr"))  validate_active_vessels_template(active_vessels) validate_effort_template(effort,effort_source ="boat_counting") # or "fisher_interview" validate_landings_template(landings) validate_active_days_template(active_days)

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: Use provided JSON specs templates ensure data consistency. Always validate datasets running analyses. Choose appropriate effort survey format according monitoring method. Keep datasets synchronized mandatory fields (e.g., year, fishing unit, landing site). Extend customize validation rules necessary national contexts.

title: Data Requirements and Validation
text: de Graaf, G. J., Nunoo, F., Ofori-Danson, P., Wiafe, G., Lamptey, E., & Bannerman, P. (2015). International training course fisheries statistics data collection. FAO Fisheries Aquaculture Circular . 1091. Rome: Food Agriculture Organization United Nations. 134 pp. Available : https://www.fao.org/3/i3639e/i3639e.pdf Stamatopoulos, C. (2002). Sample based fishery surveys: technical handbook. FAO Fisheries Technical Paper . 425. Rome: Food Agriculture Organization United Nations. 132 pp. Available : https://www.fao.org/3/y2790e/y2790e.pdf

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: vignette demonstrates apply ARTFISH methodology estimating fisheries catch effort, related indicators using artfishr R package. methodology aims extrapolate sample-based observations produce total estimates stratum sample. vignette walks computation step shows use unified workflow function artfish_compute_report(), automates full estimation process.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: package includes example datasets stored inst/extdata/samples/. Let’s load using system.file(): Following datasets required produce estimates: - Active vessels: number active vessels per stratum (per landing site) - Active days: number days month recording fishing activity - Effort survey data: fisher interviews boat counting - Landings data: catch species observed fishing trip information data structure, refer example datasets data validation requirements.
code: active_vessels <- readr::read_csv(   system.file("extdata/samples", "active_vessels.csv", package = "artfishr") ) #> Rows: 12 Columns: 6 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (1): fishing_unit #> dbl (5): year, month, minor_stratum, landing_site, fleet_engagement_number #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.  effort <- readr::read_csv(   system.file("extdata/samples", "effort.csv", package = "artfishr") ) #> Rows: 164 Columns: 9 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (2): fishing_unit, effort_fishing_duration_unit #> dbl (7): year, month, day, minor_stratum, landing_site, effort_fishing_durat... #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.  active_days <- readr::read_csv(   system.file("extdata/samples", "active_days.csv", package = "artfishr") ) #> Rows: 12 Columns: 6 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (1): fishing_unit #> dbl (5): year, month, minor_stratum, landing_site, effort_fishable_duration #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.  landings <- readr::read_csv(   system.file("extdata/samples", "landings.csv", package = "artfishr") ) #> Rows: 570 Columns: 15 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (6): fishing_trip, fishing_unit, effort_fishing_duration_unit, species, ... #> dbl (9): year, month, day, minor_stratum, landing_site, effort_fishing_durat... #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: detailed workflow showing function used artfishr compute various ARTFISH components. Indicators computed stratum sampling plan. Major minor strata must specified function define level aggregation.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: activity coefficient represents probability certain boat certain day. obtained effort survey using either fisher interviews boat counts.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: Effort estimate calculation based three components active vessels, active days activity coefficient. Effort=ActiveVessels×ActiveDays×ActivityCoefficient Effort = ActiveVessels \times ActiveDays \times ActivityCoefficient
code: effort_estimate <- artfishr::compute_effort_estimate(   active_vessels = active_vessels,   active_vessels_strategy = "latest",   landings = landings,   effort = effort,   effort_source = "fisher_interview",   active_days = active_days,   minor_strata = "minor_stratum" ) #> [1] "2025-01-01"

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: CPUE calculated landing survey data. stratum, overall average CPUE calculated.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: Catch estimate calculation based formula: Catch=CPUE×Effort Catch = CPUE \times Effort
code: catch_estimate <- artfishr::compute_catch_estimate(   effort_estimate,   landings = landings,   minor_strata = "minor_stratum" )

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: Catch estimates species obtained distributing estimated total catch according observed species composition within stratum.

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: individual steps integrated single convenience function:artfish_compute_report(). function executes complete workflow, returning structured report includes intermediate final results.
code: report <- artfishr::compute_report(   active_vessels = active_vessels,   effort = effort,   effort_source = "fisher_interview",   active_days = active_days,   active_vessels_strategy = "closest",   landings = landings,   minor_strata = "minor_stratum" ) #> [1] "2025-01-01"  # Inspect report structure str(report, max.level = 1) #> tibble [22 × 36] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)

title: Using artfishr for Small-Scale Fisheries Estimation
text: vignette illustrated: data requirements ARTFISH (active vessels, effort, active days, landings) sequential workflow used compute indicators manually integrated workflow provided artfish_compute_report() convenience production use, users adapt workflow datasets, ensuring data formats comply specifications inst/extdata/format_specs/.

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: vignette presents statistical conceptual framework underpins ARTFISH methodology implemented artfishr package. aim ARTFISH methodology produce country-level estimates catch fishing effort. sample-based approach relying stratified random sampling. ARTFISH stands Approaches, Rules Techniques Fisheries Statistical Monitoring. developed standardized methodology adaptable fisheries developing countries. design driven need provide users robust, user-friendly, reliable approaches supporting implementation cost-effective fishery statistical systems minimal external assistance. methodology developed described Constantine Stamatopoulos (Stamatopoulos, 2002) currently promoted Food Agriculture Organization United Nations (FAO).

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: ARTFISH based concept catch per unit effort (CPUE), using following relationships: CPUE=CatchEffort \mathrm{CPUE}=\frac{Catch}{Effort} Catch=CPUE×Effort \mathrm{Catch}=\mathrm{CPUE}\times\mathrm{Effort} : geographical area (stratum), reference period (e.g. one calendar month), specific fishing unit. CPUE (estimated sample) average catch per unit effort. represents average quantity fish (species combined) caught per unit effort within estimation context. Effort (estimated sample) expressed total number boat-days within estimation context. CPUE effort estimated sample data. data sources may vary depending information available country survey design implemented. Figure 1 illustrates estimation process sampling performed time space, , subset sampling days landing sites surveyed. Figure 1. ARTFISH conceptual framework.

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: statistical methods, ARTFISH estimates first computed stratum aggregated produce estimates higher levels (e.g. total national catch). first level stratification generally corresponds reference period. ARTFISH, data collection usually organised monthly basis. However, fishing seasons may appropriate calendar months contexts. selected reference period therefore defines first level stratification. Additional levels stratification can defined required: Major strata, usually based administrative, geographical, temporal criteria. Minor strata, corresponding subdivisions major stratum. Fishing units, representing lowest level stratification. Fishing units defined describe structure fisheries sector within country. Major minor strata optional defined justified local context logistical constraints. Important Population parameters always estimated lowest level stratification. Estimates higher levels obtained aggregating corresponding lower-level estimates. sample size stratum can determined using preliminary data. prior information available, following sampling effort recommended: 8 12 sampling days per month; least 30 landing surveys; least 70 effort surveys.

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: CPUE calculated data collected landing survey expressed weight catch (kg lbs) per day. Figure 2. CPUE estimation principle. estimate CPUE, following variables must collected: fishing unit (defined sampling plan); total catch weight (species combined, detailed species estimation per species); fishing trip duration (.e. time spent fishing). CPUE calculated stratum (defined combination reference period, major /minor strata, fishing unit) using following formula: CPUE=∑=1nCatchi∑=1nUnitEfforti \mathrm{CPUE}= \frac{\sum_{=1}^{n} Catch_i} {\sum_{=1}^{n} UnitEffort_i} : CatchiCatch_i total catch ii-th sampled fishing trip. UnitEffortiUnitEffort_i fishing effort associated ii-th sampled fishing trip. nn number fishing trips sampled within estimation context. ARTFISH, unit effort expressed days, corresponding fishing trip duration (time spent fishing, depending level detail collected). Consequently, CPUE formula used ARTFISH becomes: CPUE=∑=1nCatchi∑=1nTripDurationi \mathrm{CPUE}= \frac{\sum_{=1}^{n} Catch_i} {\sum_{=1}^{n} TripDuration_i} Table 1. Example landing survey records used CPUE calculation. CPUE therefore calculated : CPUE=165+0+902+1+1=63.75kg/day \mathrm{CPUE} = \frac{165+0+90}{2+1+1} = 63.75~\mathrm{kg/day} Warning Fishing trips zero catch must recorded included calculation. example, excluding fishing trips zero catch result : CPUE=165+902+1=85kg/day \mathrm{CPUE} = \frac{165+90}{2+1} = 85~\mathrm{kg/day} consequence, overall catch overestimated.

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: sampling performed time space, estimation fishing effort requires three components.  Figure 3. Effort estimation principle. : DD raising factor expressing total number days fishing activity reference period. estimated active days survey. FF raising factor expressing total number fishing units potentially operating across fishing sites within stratum. estimated frame survey vessel registry. ACAC activity coefficient. Fishing unit activity can estimated either boat activity coefficient (BACBAC), using vertical sampling (boat counts), fishing activity coefficient (FACFAC), using horizontal sampling (fisher interviews). total fishing effort therefore estimated : Effort=F×D×BAC \mathrm{Effort} = F \times D \times BAC Effort=F×D×FAC \mathrm{Effort} = F \times D \times FAC BACBAC FACFAC express probability fishing unit (boat) active given day reference period. BACBAC estimated boat activity survey, whereas FACFAC estimated fisher interviews.

what: 4.2 Number of active vessels: Estimation of the raising factor expressing the total number of active fishing units (FF)
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: raising factor FF represents number boats active within fishing fleet therefore potentially capable fishing sampling period. Depending fleet managed, FF can estimated : frame survey, boats registered; vessel registry, registration fisheries maritime administration mandatory. data sources may also considered currently explored, household surveys small-scale fisheries. stratum ii fishing unit jj, FF calculated sum number boats belonging fishing unit jj across sites included stratum: Fi,j=∑k=1qxi,j,k F_{,j} = \sum_{k=1}^{q} x_{,j,k} : xi,j,kx_{,j,k} number potentially active vessels fishing unit jj site kk within stratum ii. kk site index, ranging 11 qq. (complete required) qq total number sites within stratum ii. Fi,jF_{,j} raising factor stratum ii fishing unit jj.

what: 4.3 Number of active days: Estimation of the time raising factor (DD)
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: time raising factor DD represents total number days reference period considered normal fishing days. obtained starting total number calendar days subtracting days fishing expected occur (e.g. weekends, public holidays, bad weather). value DD estimated given estimation context (reference period, geographical stratum, fishing unit). target population therefore corresponds calendar days within reference period. stratum ii fishing unit jj, DD can calculated : Di,j=Number days month−Number non-potentially fishing days D_{,j} = \text{Number days month} - \text{Number non-potentially fishing days} Table 2. Example template active days survey. example, Table 2, Strata 1 Fishing Unit 2: D=31−9=22 D = 31 - 9 = 22 several sites belong stratum, DD calculated weighted mean site-specific values, using number boats weights. Di,j=∑m=1tDi,j,m×xi,j,m∑m=1txi,j,m D_{,j} = \frac{\sum_{m=1}^{t} D_{,j,m}\times x_{,j,m}} {\sum_{m=1}^{t} x_{,j,m}} : mm site index, ranging 11 tt. tt total number sampled sites. xi,j,mx_{,j,m} number active boats site mm stratum ii fishing unit jj. Di,j,mD_{,j,m} estimated number active fishing days site mm stratum ii fishing unit jj.

previous_headings: 4. Effort > 4.4 Activity coefficient: Estimation of the boat activity coefficient (BACBAC) or fishing activity coefficient (FACFAC)
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: activity coefficient accounts variability fishing activity among boats estimating probability fishing unit active given day. Fishing activity can estimated either boat activity coefficient (BACBAC), based vertical sampling, fishing activity coefficient (FACFAC), based horizontal sampling. total fishing effort therefore estimated : Effort=F×D×BAC \mathrm{Effort} = F \times D \times BAC Effort=F×D×FAC \mathrm{Effort} = F \times D \times FAC effort expressed boat-days CPUE expressed kg/day. Horizontal sampling. Information fishing activity collected interviewing fishers landing survey. typical question asks many days fished fixed reference period (e.g. previous 5 30 days). approach used estimate FACFAC. Vertical sampling. Fishing activity estimated counting, regular intervals, total number boats present landing sites number boats actually went fishing. approach demanding may preferable boat movements important fishing activity influenced seasonal lunar cycles (e.g. light fishing sardinellas). approach used estimate BACBAC.

previous_headings: 4. Effort > 4.4 Activity coefficient: Estimation of the boat activity coefficient (BACBAC) or fishing activity coefficient (FACFAC)
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: stratum ii fishing unit jj, BACBAC calculated dividing total number boats went fishing total number boats observed (either frame survey vessel registry): BACi,j,m=∑e=1EBoatsOuti,j,m,e∑e=1EBoatsi,j,m,e BAC_{,j,m} = \frac{\sum_{e=1}^{E} BoatsOut_{,j,m,e}} {\sum_{e=1}^{E} Boats_{,j,m,e}} : mm site index, ranging 11 tt. tt total number sampled sites. ee sampled day index, ranging 11 EE. EE total number sampled days. Boatsi,j,m,eBoats_{,j,m,e} number boats recorded site mm stratum ii fishing unit jj. BoatsOuti,j,m,eBoatsOut_{,j,m,e} number boats went fishing day ee site mm stratum ii fishing unit jj. frame survey used, formula becomes: (Formula completed.) : xi,j,mx_{,j,m} number boats site mm stratum ii fishing unit jj.

previous_headings: 4. Effort > 4.4 Activity coefficient: Estimation of the boat activity coefficient (BACBAC) or fishing activity coefficient (FACFAC)
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: simple question can added landing survey questionnaire estimate fishing activity fishing unit: many days fished last 5 days? __________ one-day fishing trips, reference period 5 7 days generally sufficient. longer fishing trips (e.g. two three weeks), appropriate use 30-day reference period. case, question may reformulated : “many days land last 30 days?” reference period defined separately fishing unit. stratum ii fishing unit jj, FACFAC calculated : FACi,j=∑b=1zDaysActivei,j,bz×RefTime FAC_{,j} = \frac{\sum_{b=1}^{z} DaysActive_{,j,b}} {z \times RefTime} : bb interview index, ranging 11 zz. zz total number interviews. DaysActivei,j,bDaysActive_{,j,b} number days interviewed fisher active reference period. RefTimeRefTime duration reference period (e.g. 5 30 days). DaysSelectedi,jDaysSelected_{,j}(check: used current formula).

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: total catch estimated, catch species obtained using following relationship: SpeciesCatch=SP×Catch SpeciesCatch = SP \times Catch : SpeciesCatchSpeciesCatch estimated catch given species within estimation context. SPSP proportion total catch represented species sampled landings. CatchCatch estimated total catch within estimation context. Using estimated fishing effort, species-specific CPUE values can also calculated.

previous_headings: 5. Total catch estimation and catch per species
title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: total catch given reference period obtained summing estimated catches across strata (fishing units major minor strata, applicable). TotalCatch=∑=1SCatchi TotalCatch = \sum_{=1}^{S} Catch_i : ii stratum index, corresponding unique combination reference period, geographical stratum, fishing unit. SS total number strata. (complete)

title: Statistical framework of the ARTFISH methodology
text: quality estimates assessed using different accuracy indicators calculated ARTFISH methodology. Two complementary approaches used assess spatial accuracy, reflects adequacy sampling effort. Temporal accuracy estimated temporal population (calendar days within reference period) considered fully defined. methodology combines algebraic probabilistic approaches evaluate accuracy CPUE activity coefficient (ACAC) estimates. following indicators produced: Spatial accuracy activity coefficient (ACAC); Temporal accuracy activity coefficient (ACAC), always equal 1 therefore require estimation; Spatial accuracy CPUE; Temporal accuracy CPUE. overall sampling accuracy defined minimum four indicators. examined first. overall accuracy low (e.g. 80%), individual accuracy indicators can used identify source highest variability.

title: ARTFISH visualisations
text: vignette describes visualisation tools available artfishr package exploring outputs computed Artfish statistical indicator, following ARTFISH methodology. visualisations organised three modules: Total Catch Effort Catch Effort Fishing Unit Catch Effort Species

title: ARTFISH visualisations
text: Total Catch Effort module provides visualisations key aggregated metrics derived Artfish computations. serves dashboard tracking overall evolution fishing activity time, ability focus specific fishing units. top-left section interface, date range selector allows user define analysis period. top-right section drop-menu select specific fishing units.  default, fishing units selected drop-menu, meaning available data aggregated. isolate specific fishing units detailed analysis, uncheck units wish exclude list.  filters summary indicators displaying total catch effort selected period fishing unit(s).  Five standardized graph boxes displayed summary indicators. box contains chart summarizing one following metrics: Total Catch Estimation Species Composition (Quantity) Total Nominal Effort Number Active Vessels Average CPUE charts shown default module loaded automatically updated new fishing unit different reporting period selected. first chart displays total catch estimation (kg) selected fishing unit(s) selected time period.  second chart displays ranking available species selected reporting period fishing unit(s), tooltips providing absolute values relative contribution total.  next row two charts displays total nominal effort (days) number active vessels selected fishing unit(s) selected reporting period.  final chart displays catch per unit effort (kg/day) selected fishing unit(s) selected reporting period.

what: Artfish - Catch and Effort by Fishing Unit
title: ARTFISH visualisations
text: Catch Effort Fishing Unit module provides dedicated workspace exploring annual Artfish results focus individual fishing units. Similar previous module, date range selector top-left section dashboard used set analysis period. drop-menu top-right section allows user select one fishing units.  filters summary indicators displaying total catch, total effort, number active vessels selected reporting period fishing unit(s).  Seven standardized graph boxes displayed summary indicators. box contains chart summarizing one following metrics: Species Composition (quantity) Fishing Unit Composition (quantity) Total Catch Estimation Catch Per Unit Effort (CPUE) Total Nominal Effort Activity Coefficient Number Active Vessels first chart displays ranking available species selected reporting period fishing unit(s). example, three fishing units selected.  second chart ranks fishing units catch quantity selected reporting period, charts containing tooltips provide absolute values relative contribution total.  third chart displays total catch estimation (kg) fishing unit selected reporting period.  fourth chart displays catch per unit effort (CPUE) fishing unit selected reporting period.  fifth chart displays total nominal effort (days) fishing unit selected reporting period.  sixth chart displays activity coefficient fishing unit selected reporting period.  final chart displays number active vessels fishing unit selected reporting period.

what: Artfish - Catch and Effort by Species
title: ARTFISH visualisations
text: Catch Effort Species module provides species-oriented workspace exploring Artfish results. initial view displays species selector containing available species database.  species selected, rest module becomes visible. Directly species selector date range selector define analysis period. fishing unit drop-menu allows user refine analysis. default, data fishing units displayed, user may also restrict view single fishing unit.  upper right panel contains two charts. left, donut chart displays distribution total catch selected species reporting period across fishing units.  right, ranking chart displays position selected species (orange) relative species, based total catch quantity. Tooltips within chart provide additional insights absolute values relative proportions.  visualisations four indicator boxes summarising key metrics selected species: Total Catch (kilograms default) Average Price (computed value catch) Total Effort Average Species Ratio  indicator boxes, three standardized graph boxes displayed module. box contains chart summarizing one following metrics: Total Catch Estimation Catch Per Unit Effort (CPUE) Total Nominal Effort charts displayed module similar described Total Catch Effort module, single species perspective.

Isolate Data Groups: Double-click group name right-hand legend show group temporarily hide others. active group name appear black text, hidden groups turn grey. Exclude Data Groups: Click group name right-hand legend hide plot. Undo Isolation/Exclusion: Click hidden (grey) group name restore plot view. Double-click plot restore original view zoomed .

text: additional set action buttons located top-right corner plot area .  left right, include: Download PNG: Saves snapshot current plot view PNG image. Zoom: Click drag bounding box directly chart zoom specific area. Pan: Click drag slide chart view horizontally vertically. Box Select: Isolate data points drawing rectangular selection box . Click icon deselect. Lasso Select: Isolate data points drawing free-form shape around . Click icon deselect. Zoom : Instantly steps zoom level closer . Zoom : Instantly steps zoom level . Autoscale: Automatically adjusts chart axes fit visible data cleanly. Reset Axes: Resets chart back default, original scale zooming panning. Plotly Link: Opens views chart within Plotly.js (v2.11.1).

title: Introduction to artfishr package
code: # Load sample datasets active_vessels <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/samples/active_vessels.csv", package = "artfishr")) effort <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/samples/effort.csv", package = "artfishr")) landings <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/samples/landings.csv", package = "artfishr")) active_days <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/samples/active_days.csv", package = "artfishr"))  # Validate data  validate_input_datasets(   active_vessels = active_vessels,   effort = effort,   effort_source = "fisher_interview",   landings = landings,   active_days = active_days )  #Run artfish report <- artfishr::compute_report(   active_vessels = active_vessels,   effort = effort,   effort_source = "fisher_interview",   active_days = active_days,   active_vessels_strategy = "closest",   landings = landings,   minor_strata = "minor_stratum" )  #See result str(report, max.level = 1)

title: ARTFISH Computation - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_computation_server
text: id Character string. Module namespace identifier. refresh refresher computation effort effort data effort_source Character string indicating type effort source. active_vessels active vessels data active_vessels_strategy active vessels strategy active_days active days landings landings minor_strata Character string targeting column name considered minor strata. progress_fn function monitor progress

what: ARTFISH Fishing Unit - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_fishing_unit_server
title: ARTFISH Fishing Unit - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_fishing_unit_server
text: Server-side logic ARTFISH Fishing Unit dashboard. *"Catch effort - Detailed fishing unit"* module provides dedicated workspace exploring key output metrics derived artfish_compute_report, focus one multiple fishing units. enables users analyse evolution fishing activity time, filtering options fishing units time range. module composed : selector panel adjust time range fishing units display set KPIs presenting aggregated metrics selected period Three boxed plots powered fdishinyr::generic_chart allowing users analyse ranked top items : (1) Species composition (quantity) (2) Fishing unit composition (quantity) (3) Fishing unit composition (value) Six boxed plots powered fdishinyr::generic_chart allowing users analyse key Artfish metrics: (1) Total catch estimation (2) Catch per unit effort (CPUE) (3) Total nominal effort (4) Activity coefficient (5) Number active vessels (6) Total value estimation

title: ARTFISH Fishing Unit - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_fishing_unit_server
code: artfish_shiny_fishing_unit_server(   id,   lang = NULL,   estimate,   effort_source,   minor_strata = NULL,   opts = list() )

title: ARTFISH Fishing Unit - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_fishing_unit_server
text: id Character string. Module namespace identifier. lang Optional language parameter. Can either: character string (static language), reactive returning character string (dynamic language) NULL, current global language used. Default NULL estimate reactive data frame aggregating output artfish_compute, enriched human-readable labels. effort_source Reactive haracter string indicating type effort source. Must either "fisher_interview" "boat_counting". minor_strata Reactive haracter string targeting column name considered minor strata. activated opts named list options. now limited : - refresh_ui gives capacity inject refresh UI button (dynamic computation)

what: ARTFISH Overview - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_overview_server
title: ARTFISH Overview - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_overview_server
text: Server-side logic ARTFISH Overview dashboard. *"Total catch effort - Statistics country level"* module provides high-level visualization key output metrics derived artfish_compute_report. acts dashboard track overall evolution fishing activity time, ability filter fishing units time range. module composed : selector panel adjust time range fishing units display set KPIs presenting aggregated country-level metrics selected period Seven boxed plots powered fdishinyr::generic_chart allowing users analyse: (1) Total catch estimation (2) Species composition (quantity) (3) Total nominal effort (4) Number active vessels (5) Total value estimation (6) Species composition (value) (7) Catch per unit effort (CPUE)

what: ARTFISH Report - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_report_server
title: ARTFISH Report - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_report_server
text: Server-side logic ARTFISH Report dashboard. *"Detailed results Artfish computation – stratum"* module provides ergonomic view key intermediate results calculation steps underlying Artfish algorithm given temporal unit. initial view displays selector inviting user choose year. year available, user must first compute least one indicator using artfish_compute_report. Successive dropdown lists allow user select month fishing unit submitting selection. complete report displayed right-hand side. module composed : selector panel choose report display selecting year, month, fishing unit button validate selection set KPIs presenting report status type effort source gauge chart presenting overall accuracy quality indicator ergonomic report view including intermediate steps final indicators estimation : (1) Effort (2) Landings (3) Estimated catch species

title: ARTFISH Report - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_report_server
code: artfish_shiny_report_server(   id,   lang = NULL,   estimate,   effort_source,   minor_strata )

title: ARTFISH Report - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_report_server
text: id Character string. Module namespace identifier. lang Optional language parameter. Can either: character string (static language), reactive expression returning character string (dynamic language) NULL, current global language used. Default NULL. estimate data frame aggregating output artfish_compute, enriched human-readable labels. effort_source Character string indicating type effort source. Must either "fisher_interview" "boat_counting". minor_strata Character string targeting column name considered minor strata.

what: ARTFISH Species - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_species_server
title: ARTFISH Species - Shiny Server Module — artfish_shiny_species_server
text: Server-side logic ARTFISH Species dashboard. *"Catch effort - Detailed species"* module provides species-oriented workspace exploring key output metrics derived artfish_compute_report, focus individual species caught. initial view displays selector inviting user choose species. dropdown lists species entries available database. Users can analyse evolution fishing activity time, filtering options species, fishing units, time range. module composed : selector panel choose species display selector panel adjust time range fishing units display Two boxed plots powered fdishinyr::generic_chart allowing users analyse, species level: (1) Fishing unit composition (quantity) (2) Target species ranking (quantity) set KPIs presenting aggregated metrics selected period Five boxed plots powered fdishinyr::generic_chart allowing users analyse key Artfish metrics: (1) Total catch estimation (2) Catch per unit effort (CPUE) (3) Total value estimation (4) Average price (5) Total nominal effort

code: artfish_shiny_species_server(   id,   lang = NULL,   estimate,   effort_source = NULL,   minor_strata = NULL,   opts = list() )

Default NULL estimate reactive data frame aggregating output artfish_compute, enriched human-readable labels. effort_source Reactive character string indicating type effort source. Must either "fisher_interview" "boat_counting". activated minor_strata Reactive character string targeting column name considered minor strata. activated opts named list options. now limited : - refresh_ui gives capacity inject refresh UI button (dynamic computation)

title: Computes CPUE — compute_cpue
text: CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) computed landings, ratio nominal landed catches (catch_nominal_landed) effort fishing duration (effort_fishing_duration). computation performed grouped strata compound year, month fishing_unit. strata can extended additional columns minor_strata argument. Since landings give details data landed species, nominal landed catches sum grouped strata fishing trip get total nominal landed catches fishing trip. catch_nominal_landed effort_fishing_duration sum strata CPUE computed ratio sum. Note: Additional checks performed remove data NAs.

title: Computes CPUE — compute_cpue
text: landings landings minor_strata minor_strata. Default NULL

title: Computes CPUE — compute_cpue
text: tibble object giving CPUE strata

title: Computes effort activity coefficient — compute_effort_activity_coefficient
text: activity coefficient computed effort. Depending source effort data, source information used different: - case 'fisher_interview' coefficient activity computed ratio effort_fishing_duration effort_fishing_reference_period. - case 'boat_counting' coefficient activity computed ratio fleet_engagement_number fleet_engagement_max computation performed grouped strata compound minimum year, month fishing_unit. strata can extended adding one columns minor_strata argument. Note: Additional check performed remove data NAs, ensure data consistency

title: Computes nominal effort estimate — compute_effort_estimate
text: Computes nominal effort estimate

title: Computes nominal effort estimate — compute_effort_estimate
code: compute_effort_estimate(   effort,   effort_source = c("fisher_interview", "boat_counting", "household_interview"),   landings,   active_days = NULL,   active_vessels = NULL,   active_vessels_strategy = c("latest", "closest"),   census_typology = NULL,   minor_strata = NULL,   progress_fn = NULL )

title: Computes nominal effort estimate — compute_effort_estimate_with_FAO_Artfish
text: effort estimate computed product fleet_engagement_number (derived active_vessels), effort_fishable_duration (derivated active_days, effort_activity_coefficient computed compute_effort_activity_coefficient function. computation nominal effort estimate done grouped strata compound year, month fishing_unit. strata can extended additional columns minor_strata argument. Active vessels: active_vessels can either time-dependent,ie given year/month, year atemporal, mainly depending frequency vessels census/survey operated country. additional argument called active_vessels_strategy controls select  active_vessels data time, data time-dependent. latest strategy select latest data acquired time, closest select closest data acquired time, data acquired effort data considered. example, vessel census performed five years, data available 2007 2012. Effort data 2011 needs computed. active_vessels data used? case latest strategy, 2011, latest data available 2007, one used. case closest strategy, 2011, used closest vessel data (ie 2012), assuming betters characterizes fleet engagement year considered. Active days: case 'fisher_interview' (source effort data), active_days data ignored, days month used (generated automatically function)

title: Computes nominal effort estimate — compute_effort_estimate_with_FAO_Artfish
code: compute_effort_estimate_with_FAO_Artfish(   effort,   effort_source = c("fisher_interview", "boat_counting"),   landings,   active_days = NULL,   active_vessels,   active_vessels_strategy = c("latest", "closest"),   minor_strata = NULL,   progress_fn = NULL )

title: Computes nominal effort estimate — compute_effort_estimate_with_FAO_Artfish
text: effort effort data effort_source effort_source (register_interview / boat_counting) landings landings active_days active_days. Default NULL (auto-generated) active_vessels active vessels active_vessels_strategy strategy associate active vessels effort based time. Active vessels period match necessarily periods data (effort, landings), can reported either year year/month. parameter let decide methodology used select active vessels based time. can either "latest" (taking latest period), "closest" ie closest active vessels time, data period. case 2 periods /equally closer, latest time data period taken. minor_strata minor_strata. Default NULL progress_fn progress function args (, n, label). Default NULL

title: Select active vessels — select_active_vessels
text: Select active vessels selection years Active vessels: active_vessels can either time-dependent,ie given year/month, year atemporal, mainly depending frequency vessels census/survey operated country. additional argument called active_vessels_strategy controls select  active_vessels data time, data time-dependent. latest strategy select latest data acquired time, closest select closest data acquired time, data acquired effort data considered. example, vessel census performed five years, data available 2007 2012. Effort data 2011 needs computed. active_vessels data used? case latest strategy, 2011, latest data available 2007, one used. case closest strategy, 2011, used closest vessel data (ie 2012), assuming betters characterizes fleet engagement year considered.

title: Select active vessels — select_active_vessels
text: periods periods, list unique years year/month periods active_vessels active vessels object class tibble active_vessels_strategy strategy associate active vessels effort based time. Active vessels period match necessarily periods data (effort, landings), can reported either year year/month. parameter let decide methodology used select active vessels based time. can either "latest" (taking latest period), "closest" ie closest active vessels time, data period. case 2 periods /equally closer, latest time data period taken.
